generated_comment: Bro, honestly, what do these girls even want? Everyone knows he's married, still she goes and hooks up. Ghar todne wali. Bechara bande ko phasaya hoga pakka. So typical of some women.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment blames the woman entirely for an affair, labeling her a 'ghar todne wali' (homewrecker) and suggesting she 'trapped' the man ('phasaya hoga'), while implying the man was a 'bechara' (helpless victim). This exemplifies homewrecker framing and male victimhood, where the woman is seen as a seductress responsible for destroying a family.
original_frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 23. Single. A college student in Bengaluru. You are in a WhatsApp group with seven male college friends. Someone has shared a meme that says women spend two hours putting on makeup and then get angry when men call them fake. You are reacting to the meme and adding your own comment about women who wear heavy makeup and what they are hiding and whether men have a right to know what a woman actually looks like before committing to a relationship.
